audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in ldapexop.c are properly guarded: `ld` is guaranteed non-NULL because `tool_conn_setup(0,0)` calls `exit()` on `ldap_initialize` failure (never returns NULL); `prog` is guaranteed non-NULL from `lutil_progname`; all `argv[n]` accesses are bounded by argc checks; `retoid`/`retdata` are guarded by explicit NULL checks before dereferencing; and `ber_dupbv` safely handles NULL `bv_val` (verified in memory.c:500-502). No NULL pointer dereference path exists within ldapexop.c.
